title: multidevice: relay client map supports N connections per pub_id
description:
Refactor relay/src/state.rs to allow multiple simultaneous connections from the same pub_id (different devices).

Why: currently clients: DashMap<String, (ClientTx, u64)> maps pub_id to a single connection channel. A second device connecting with the same pub_id overwrites the first, dropping delivery to the first device.

What to do:
- Change clients to DashMap<String, Vec<(ClientTx, u64)>>
- connect(): append to the vec instead of replacing; assign a monotonically increasing seq per connection as before
- disconnect(): remove only the specific ClientTx from the vec (match by channel identity or seq); remove the key entirely when vec is empty
- deliver_live(): iterate all channels for the target pub_id, attempt delivery to each, return true if any succeeded
- broadcast(): flatten all device channels across all pub_ids when collecting targets
- broadcast_to_group(): same — flatten per-pub_id vecs when building the delivery list
- connection_seq(): return minimum seq across all devices for a pub_id (lowest = admin, consistent with user-level admin election)

Acceptance: two connections from the same pub_id both receive delivered messages; disconnect of one does not affect the other; all existing relay integration tests pass.
close_reason: DashMap refactored to Vec per pub_id; connect returns seq; disconnect removes by seq; connection_seq returns min; deliver_live fans out to all channels; all tests pass
